LLMBehaviorError: llm.rate_limited: API Error: Server is temporarily limiting requests (not your usage limit) · Rate limited

What failed:
  The LLM provider rejected the request as rate-limited:
    API Error: Server is temporarily limiting requests (not your usage limit) · Rate limited

Why:
  Providers cap requests per minute / tokens per minute. When the cap is hit, retries within the rate-limit window will fail again — the framework refuses to silently retry-loop without an explicit operator decision, because a long retry loop can quietly exhaust the behavior's budget.

How to fix:
  Wait until the rate-limit window resets (provider-specific — usually 60 seconds), then re-run. For long-running goals, set a higher `max_seconds` budget so the runtime tolerates intermittent rate-limits. If this happens during fork/replay, the cache hit should normally prevent the live call — check whether the prompt hash matches the recorded one.

More:
  https://docs.activegraph.ai/errors/llm-behavior-error

API Error: Server is temporarily limiting requests (not your usage limit) · Rate limited

LLMBehaviorError: llm.rate_limited: API Error: Server is temporarily limiting requests (not your usage limit) · Rate limited

What failed:
  The LLM provider rejected the request as rate-limited:
    API Error: Server is temporarily limiting requests (not your usage limit) · Rate limited

Why:
  Providers cap requests per minute / tokens per minute. When the cap is hit, retries within the rate-limit window will fail again — the framework refuses to silently retry-loop without an explicit operator decision, because a long retry loop can quietly exhaust the behavior's budget.

How to fix:
  Wait until the rate-limit window resets (provider-specific — usually 60 seconds), then re-run. For long-running goals, set a higher `max_seconds` budget so the runtime tolerates intermittent rate-limits. If this happens during fork/replay, the cache hit should normally prevent the live call — check whether the prompt hash matches the recorded one.

More:
  https://docs.activegraph.ai/errors/llm-behavior-error

  Wait until the rate-limit window resets (provider-specific — usually 60 seconds), then re-run. payload.completion_evidence: blocked: Synthetic test probe (failure_summary="Synthetic diff-happy-path test", failure_event_id=evt_synth_for_diff_happy, dedup_key=diff-happy-test-*) — no real bug exists to diagnose. Proposing a fabricated diff just to exercise Phoenix's apply→test→commit path would pollute the inner-repo audit trail with a meaningless commit attributed to a non-existent failure, violating the discipline rule "never produce evidence for a result that wasn't earned." If the diff happy-path needs end-to-end validation, the correct fix is Phoenix-side: add a `--probe` mode that loads a checked-in fixture diff from `frames/phoenix-probes/` (e.g. a known-trivial docstring patch), applies+tests it in a fresh worktree, and asserts the worktree path executed — without committing/pushing or routing through an agent. That tests the pipeline mechanics without manufacturing fake bugs. Also: this probe should have carried `extras.synthetic=true` so Sasha's pt.5 short-circuit (`scripts/sasha-skeptic.mjs::routeFailureToAgent`) would have absorbed it before Phoenix ever created a todo — the fact that it reached me suggests the emitter bypassed `emitSyntheticProbe()`. Recommend wiring all synthetic flywheel probes through that helper.
